_comment: Canonical registry of paper source IDs used in @source annotations throughout ntn-sim-core.
_format: PAP-YYYY-LABEL: each entry maps to one reviewed paper; STD-*: 3GPP/ITU standards; ASSUME-*: explicit modeling assumptions.

PAP-2021-SHADOWED-RICIAN.title: A Statistical Model for the Land Mobile Satellite Channel and Its Application to Nongeostationary Orbit Systems

PAP-2022-A4EVENT-CORE.title: Handover Management in LEO Satellite Networks (A4-event baseline)
PAP-2022-A4EVENT-CORE.topic: A4 event trigger threshold, hysteresis, TTT parameters

PAP-2022-SINR-ELEVATION.title: SINR and Elevation Threshold Co-design for LEO Handover

PAP-2022-SENSORS-BH.title: Three-Dimensional Resource Allocation for Beam Hopping in LEO Satellite Networks
PAP-2022-SENSORS-BH.topic: Beam geometry and implementation losses (0.5 dB feeder + 2.0 dB pointing)

PAP-2024-BEAM-MGMT-SPECTRUM.title: Beam Management and Spectrum Reuse in Multi-Beam LEO
PAP-2024-BEAM-MGMT-SPECTRUM.topic: FRF reuse-7 geometry, hex beam layout

PAP-2024-HDMMA-MOBILITY.title: Handover Decision-Making with Mobility Awareness for LEO NTN
PAP-2024-HDMMA-MOBILITY.topic: UE mobility model integration with handover trigger

PAP-2024-HOBS.title: Handover Optimization for Beam-Switching in LEO Networks (HOBS)
PAP-2024-HOBS.topic: Walker(55°,72,6,F=1), 550km, Ka-band 28GHz, 100MHz BW, 37 beams, FRF=3, Pmax=50dBm, gamma_thr=10dB

PAP-2024-MADRL-CORE.title: Multi-Agent DRL for LEO Satellite Beam Hopping Scheduling
PAP-2024-MADRL-CORE.topic: BH scheduling MADRL baseline, beam slot assignment

PAP-2024-MCCHO-CORE.title: MC-CHO: Multi-Connectivity Conditional Handover for LEO NTN
PAP-2024-MCCHO-CORE.topic: MC-HO dual-active phase, packet duplication, CHO comparison

PAP-2024-MORL-MULTIBEAM.title: Handover for Multi-Beam LEO Satellite Networks: A Multi-Objective Reinforcement Learning Method
PAP-2024-MORL-MULTIBEAM.topic: MODQN baseline: 4 satellites, 780 km, 7 beams/satellite, 100 users, 20 GHz, 500 MHz, 2 W per link, three-objective reward, weights [0.5,0.3,0.2]

PAP-2025-DAPS-CORE.title: DAPS Handover for LEO Non-Terrestrial Networks
PAP-2025-DAPS-CORE.topic: DAPS dual-active procedure, path switch timing, zero-interruption HO

PAP-2025-DIST-BH-HETERO.title: Distributed Beam Hopping in Heterogeneous LEO Constellations
PAP-2025-DIST-BH-HETERO.topic: BH slot distribution, heterogeneous satellite power

PAP-2025-DYNLHT-VELOCITY.title: Dynamic LEO Handover Threshold Adaption via Satellite Velocity

PAP-2025-EAQL.title: Energy-Aware Q-Learning for LEO Handover Optimization (EAQL)
PAP-2025-EAQL.topic: Energy cost per HO, Q-learning reward function

PAP-2025-EEBH-UPLINK.title: Energy-Efficient Beam Hopping for Uplink LEO NTN
PAP-2025-EEBH-UPLINK.topic: Uplink BH energy model, slot-level energy accounting

PAP-2025-MAAC-BHPOWER.title: Multi-Agent Actor-Critic for Beam Hopping Power Allocation in LEO
PAP-2025-MAAC-BHPOWER.topic: Joint BH pattern + power allocation, MAAC architecture

PAP-2025-MADQN-HO.title: MADQN-based Handover for Multi-Satellite LEO Networks
PAP-2025-MADQN-HO.topic: Multi-agent DQN handover, shared observation space

PAP-2025-RSMA.title: Rate Splitting Multiple Access for GEO+LEO Soft Handover

PAP-2025-SMASH-MADQL.title: SMASH: Satellite Multi-Agent SINR-Aware Handover (MADQL)
PAP-2025-SMASH-MADQL.topic: MADQL multi-satellite coordination, energy efficiency metric

PAP-2025-TIMERCHO-CORE.title: Timer-CHO: Geometry-Assisted Conditional Handover for LEO NTN
PAP-2025-TIMERCHO-CORE.topic: Timer-CHO α factor, ToS_remain geometry, L3 IIR filter k

PAP-2026-BHFREQREUSE.title: Beam Hopping with Frequency Reuse Optimization in LEO

PAP-2026-DRL-BHOPT.title: DRL-based Beam Hopping Optimization for LEO Satellite Systems

PAP-2025-JCAP-LEO.title: Joint Channel Allocation and Power Control for Multi-Beam LEO Satellites
PAP-2025-JCAP-LEO.topic: FR3 frequency reuse factor, multi-beam interference management

STD-IEEE-T0.title: Standard reference temperature for thermal noise calculations

STD-3GPP-38331.title: NR; Radio Resource Control (RRC) protocol specification

STD-3GPP-38133.title: NR; Requirements for support of radio resource management

STD-3GPP-38811.title: Study on New Radio (NR) to support non-terrestrial networks

STD-3GPP-38811-LOS-20DEG.title: LOS elevation threshold 20° — simplified approximation of TR 38.811 §6.7 LOS probability function
STD-3GPP-38811-LOS-20DEG.note: The 20° single-threshold approximation replaces the continuous probability function P_LOS(α) with a binary decision: elevation ≥ 20° → LOS; <20° → NLOS. This is a conventional simulator simplification used across multiple corpus papers. The exact value 20° is not normatively mandated but is consistent with §6.7 minimum operational elevation angle guidance.

STD-3GPP-38821.title: Solutions for NR to support non-terrestrial networks

STD-ITU-P676.title: Attenuation by atmospheric gases and related effects

STD-ITU-P618.title: Propagation data and prediction methods required for Earth-space systems

STD-ITU-S672.title: Satellite antenna radiation pattern for use as a design objective

STD-3GPP-38811-TABLE-4.4-1.title: Study on New Radio (NR) to support non-terrestrial networks — UE noise figure table
relevantSections: Table 4.4-1: handheld UE NF = 9 dB; laptop/VSAT NF = 5 dB
STD-3GPP-38811-TABLE-4.4-1.note: Handheld profiles (S-band case 9) use NF=9 dB; Ka-band VSAT-like profiles use NF=5 dB

3GPP-NTN-ACCESS.title: 3GPP NTN access baseline — channel model and minimum elevation
relevantSections: TR 38.811 §6.6 (FSPL, shadow fading, clutter loss by deployment environment)
3GPP-NTN-ACCESS.note: Used as sourceMap id '3GPP-NTN-ACCESS' in profile sourceMap entries; covers minimum elevation, deployment-environment-dependent SF/CL, and NTN channel tier selection

ASSUME-ORB-001.description: Walker constellation: circular orbit, equal spacing, phasing factor F per 3GPP TR 38.821 §5.2

ASSUME-ORB-002.description: SGP4 propagation uses satellite.js; atmospheric drag model B* from TLE

ASSUME-ORB-003.description: Ground track velocity approximated as v = sqrt(μ/r); used for Doppler and ToS estimates

assumptions.ASSUME-CHAN-001: Path loss Tier 1–6 applied sequentially; each tier adds to total loss in dB

ASSUME-BEAM-001.description: Beam footprint radius = slant-range × tan(half-angle); off-axis angle uses spherical law of cosines

ASSUME-BEAM-002.description: FRF group assignment is deterministic (beam index mod FRF); no dynamic frequency planning

assumptions.ASSUME-HO-001: Hard-HO / A3 / A4 / D2 / CHO interruption is approximated by RTT = 2 x one-way propagation delay; DAPS and MC-HO use 0 ms interruption because the dual-active path is maintained during execution
assumptions.ASSUME-HO-DAPS: DAPS dual-active phase bounded by max_dual_sec; packet duplication optional

ASSUME-MODQN-ORBIT.description: PAP-2024-MORL-MULTIBEAM fixes only a 4-satellite, 780 km STK constellation, not the detailed plane layout, inclination, or epoch. The runtime uses a disclosed 2x2 Walker-style proxy shell centered on the paper's Beijing study area so MODQN work can proceed on stable contracts without fabricating paper-backed orbital literals.

ASSUME-MODQN-BEAM.description: PAP-2024-MORL-MULTIBEAM specifies 7 beams per satellite and per-beam channel gains but does not specify the antenna family, peak gain, footprint diameter, or reuse-plan implementation needed by the current simulator. The runtime uses a disclosed 7-beam hexagonal proxy with explicit gain/footprint values and FRF=1, while keeping zero interference in the baseline scope.

ASSUME-MODQN-RUNTIME.description: MODQN baseline runtime bridging assumptions that are required by the current platform but absent from the paper: a disclosed episode epoch that yields an in-view pass for the 10 s runtime window, permissive attach floor, hard handover with TTT=0 and hysteresis=0, a diagnostic ping-pong window, and direct mapping of per-user decisions onto the independent-handover runtime path. These settings are runtime mechanics, not paper-backed baseline constants.

assumptions.ASSUME-ATM-001: Tier 4 atmospheric loss uses fixed mid-latitude zenith estimates from ITU-R simplified models: gaseous 0.35 dB (10–25 GHz) / 0.6 dB (25+ GHz) from ITU-R P.676; rain 0.8 dB (10–25 GHz) / 1.5 dB (25+ GHz) from ITU-R P.618 at 99% availability; scintillation 0.4 dB Ka-band from ITU-R P.618 tropospheric. Claim scope: LEO NTN Ka-band baseline only; not for site-specific or availability-sensitive studies. Source: link-budget.ts Tier 4 block.
assumptions.ASSUME-ENERGY-001: Layer 1 beam-state energy defaults: txPowerPerBeamDbm=40 dBm (= 10 W per beam, aligned with spec P1 per PAP-2025-MAAC-BHPOWER [S10]; legacy value was 43 dBm — do not use); activeBeamPowerW=20, idlePowerW=5, offBeamPowerW=0.1 are unverified calibration values and must not be labeled paper-backed. EP1 semantic rule: runtime systemEeBitsPerJoule is active-TX-only EE, while runtime totalPowerW is a broader beam-state communication-power proxy; neither may be repackaged as total communication power without explicit disclosure.

ASSUME-HO-ENERGY-001.description: Per-handover energy cost E_u,HO has no corpus-backed numeric default in the final simulator spec. If energy_per_handover_j is set, it must be labeled assumption-backed, provenance-tagged at parameterPath=energy.energy_per_handover_j, and reported as a scenario sensitivity parameter rather than a paper-backed baseline constant.

ASSUME-ENE-001.description: Layer 2 energy: solar panel output constant during eclipse = 0; beta angle determines eclipse fraction

ASSUME-RF-001.description: Carrier frequency assumption: Ka-band 20 GHz used as representative value for BH resource-allocation studies where no single corpus paper specifies the exact downlink frequency. Must be swept in frequency-sensitive experiments. NF and noise temperature are covered by STD-3GPP-38811-TABLE-4.4-1 and ASSUME-CUR-002 respectively.

ASSUME-RF-002.description: Per-beam TX power dBm: tx_power_per_beam_dbm=40dBm satellite-side per-beam P_tx reference; consistent with max_tx_power_dbm aggregate cap. No single corpus paper mandates this exact value — derived from system-level P_tx/P_EIRP budget anchored by PAP-2025-MAAC-BHPOWER.

ASSUME-CUR-002.description: Thermal noise T_sys = T_ant + T_0*(NF-1); T_ant = 290 K (clear sky); T_0 = 290 K

assumptions.ASSUME-TRAJECTORY-CACHE-PRECOMPUTE: Satellite positions pre-computed at 0.5 s intervals; interpolated cubically between ticks

ASSUME-MEO-BASELINE.description: MEO cross-orbit comparison baseline: O3b-like circular orbit ~8062 km equatorial, Ka-band, single beam per satellite

ASSUME-GEO-BASELINE.description: GEO cross-orbit comparison baseline: 35786 km geostationary, shadow fraction near-zero except equinox season (β ≈ 0°)

assumptions.ASSUME-ORB-REPRO-RT1: RT-1 (PAP-2022-SINR-ELEVATION) reproduction: Walker F=1 phasing used; paper does not specify exact epoch or phasing factor
assumptions.ASSUME-ORB-REPRO-RT2: RT-2 (PAP-2024-HOBS) reproduction: Walker F=1 phasing used; paper does not specify exact epoch
assumptions.ASSUME-ORB-REPRO-RT3: RT-3 (PAP-2025-TIMERCHO-CORE) reproduction: Walker 24×22 used as Starlink-like proxy; paper does not mandate exact constellation layout
assumptions.ASSUME-TIMERCHO-GEOM: Timer-CHO geometry: α×TTT approximation used for remaining time estimate; full ToS_remain model (requires per-beam radius and satellite velocity) is beyond current HO manager scope

ASSUME-HO-TTT-NTN.description: NTN-extended TTT=640 ms: all canonical profiles use ttt_ms=640 as a representative 3GPP NTN value. Rationale: spec H2 lists paper-backed presets of 0/40/256 ms (PAP-2022-A4EVENT-CORE uses 40 ms); the NTN context adds propagation delay compensation overhead (~2×one-way delay ≈ 2×4 ms at 600 km). 640 ms is used as a conservative NTN baseline that was common in early 3GPP NTN study-item discussions, but no specific corpus paper assigns exactly 640 ms as the default. Claim scope: assumption-backed placeholder for NTN TTT; must be swept in sensitivity analysis for handover-timing-sensitive experiments. Spec mode: Advanced (not Realistic per H2). Do not present 640 ms as a paper-backed fixed default in thesis comparison tables.

ASSUME-HO-THRESHOLD-SINR.description: SINR-relative trigger threshold: all profiles use trigger_threshold_db=−6 dB as a SINR-relative handover threshold. This is a design-level simplification vs spec H3, which specifies an A4 absolute power threshold derived as noise_floor_dBm + Q_out. The −6 dB SINR form is an engineering choice that avoids maintaining a noise-floor estimate in the trigger path; no corpus paper explicitly uses −6 dB SINR as the A4 threshold in this relative form. Claim scope: assumption-backed SINR gating value; must be declared in thesis as a simplification of the full H3 derivation. Spec mode: Advanced (manual setting; Realistic requires derivation per H3).

assumptions.ASSUME-SINR-LOS-THRESHOLD: DEPRECATED legacy alias for the LOS-threshold provenance entry. New sourceMap entries must use STD-3GPP-38811-LOS-20DEG instead. This ASSUME-* id predates the later reclassification of the 20° LOS threshold as a standard-backed approximation of 3GPP TR 38.811 §6.7. Keep only for backward audit traceability; do not use in new Realistic profiles or new UI/schema exposure logic.

ASSUME-BW-001.description: 500 MHz bandwidth: representative value for BH resource-allocation studies; no single corpus paper mandates this exact value; must be swept as sensitivity parameter in BW-sensitive experiments

ASSUME-BH-001.description: BH frame structure defaults: bh_max_active_per_slot, bh_frame_duration_sec, bh_slots_per_frame are engineering choices derived from beam count geometry (ceil(num_beams/max_active)); no single corpus paper mandates these exact values; must be documented as assumption-backed in any benchmark that reports slot-level scheduling results

ASSUME-BH-DAPS-001.description: Beam hopping frame structure for earth-moving DAPS profile: bh_max_active_per_slot=7 (ceil(19/3)), bh_slots_per_frame=3, bh_frame_duration_sec=5, round-robin strategy; engineering choice to demonstrate beam scheduling combined with DAPS dual-active handover; no corpus paper combines beam hopping + DAPS

ASSUME-HO-002.description: Handover engineering parameters without paper-backed defaults: sinr_ema_alpha (SINR IIR filter coefficient), d2_serving_dist_km / d2_target_dist_km (D2 distance-based HO thresholds), pingPongWindowSec (ping-pong suppression window), cho_offset_db (CHO trigger offset). Values used in profiles are engineering choices; no single corpus paper provides these exact defaults. Must be declared as assumption-backed in any result that depends on these values.

ASSUME-HO-TTT-A3.description: A3 TTT=3000ms for earth-moving beam proactive handover: requires 3 consecutive ticks (stepSec=1s) of sustained candidate superiority; modeled after leo-beam-sim 3.5s trigger-time validated implementation; compensates for beam dwell time ~7s at 600km.

ASSUME-HO-SINR-EMA.description: SINR EMA smoothing coefficient α=0.3 (equivalent to 3GPP L3 filter k≈7): suppresses single-tick SINR fluctuations in serving measurement before HO evaluation; time constant ≈2.6s at stepSec=1s. Engineering choice within ASSUME-HO-002 envelope.

ASSUME-HO-PP-GUARD.description: Ping-pong guard window 30s: blocks TTT start when best HO target is the previously-served satellite and last HO was within 30s. Standard stability mechanism in NTN handover literature (PAP-2021-SESSION-DURATION documents LEO dwell times of 40-180s). Engineering choice within ASSUME-HO-002 envelope.

ASSUME-HO-DAPS-OVERLAP.description: DAPS dual-active window extended to 3.0 s for the 1 s discrete runtime so prepared/dual-active execution can complete before fallback; keeps overlap visible without forcing long low-elevation source retention.

ASSUME-HO-DAPS-PREP-ELEV.description: DAPS TTT accelerant threshold 30°: when serving elevation drops below this value, effective TTT is scaled down (50–100%), accelerating handover completion at low elevation. NOT a trigger gate — handover preparation uses pure A3-style (candidate > serving + hysteresis) regardless of elevation. 10° remains the hard release floor.

ASSUME-UE-001.description: UE scenario parameters without universal paper-backed defaults: ueConfig.speed_kmh (static UE assumed as baseline unless mobility study is the topic), ueConfig.count (100 is a common simulation population in corpus papers but not mandated). These are scenario-level engineering choices unless a specific paper explicitly constrains them.

ASSUME-TRAFFIC-001.description: BH traffic arrival rate: bh_traffic_arrival_rate default (10 arrivals/beam/s Poisson) is an engineering placeholder; no single corpus paper mandates this exact arrival intensity. Must be swept in any traffic-sensitive BH experiment.

LEO-BEAM-SIM-REF.description: Donor implementation reference: sinr-offset handover policy parameters (offset_db=3, trigger_time=3.5s, pending_hold=1.5s, smoothing=0.5s, intra_switch=0.75s) ported from project/leo-beam-sim. These are engineering design choices validated in the donor app, not paper-backed constants. Must be declared as assumption-backed in any result that depends on these values.
